facts.text: UConn's women's basketball team won 111 consecutive games from 2014 to 2017, the longest winning streak in Division I basketball history.
Pat Summitt coached Tennessee for 38 seasons and compiled a record of 1,098 wins and 208 losses.
The first NCAA Division I Women's Basketball Tournament was held in 1982, with Louisiana Tech defeating Cheyney State 76-62.
Caitlin Clark became the NCAA Division I all-time leading scorer — men's or women's — surpassing Pete Maravick's long-standing record in 2024.
Title IX, enacted in 1972, prohibited sex-based discrimination in federally funded education programs, transforming women's college athletics.
Geno Auriemma has coached UConn to 11 national championships, the most by any coach in women's college basketball history.
Diana Taurasi went 39-0 in her senior season at UConn in 2003-04, winning her third consecutive national title.
The 2024 NCAA Women's Tournament championship game between South Carolina and Iowa drew 18.9 million viewers, the most-watched basketball game — men's or women's — on ESPN.
Dawn Staley won three national championships as head coach of South Carolina (2017, 2022, 2024) after a Hall of Fame playing career at Virginia.
Pat Summitt was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's disease in 2011 and passed away on June 28, 2016, at age 64.
Louisiana Tech won the first two NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament titles in 1982 and 1988 under coach Sonja Hogg and Leon Barmore.
Cheryl Miller scored 105 points in a high school game before dominating at USC, where she won two national titles (1983, 1984).
Maya Moore won four national championships at UConn (2009, 2010, 2011, 2013) and was named the AP Player of the Year twice.
Breanna Stewart won four consecutive national titles at UConn from 2013 to 2016 and was named Most Outstanding Player of the Final Four all four years.
The UConn Huskies went undefeated in four separate seasons: 1994-95, 2001-02, 2008-09, and 2009-10.
Baylor's Brittney Griner was a dominant force in college basketball, leading the Bears to a national championship in 2012 and recording 748 career blocked shots.
South Carolina went 38-0 in the 2023-24 season to win the national championship, becoming the tenth team to finish undefeated.
The AIAW (Association for Intercollegiate Athletics for Women) governed women's college sports before the NCAA took over women's championships in 1982.
Kim Mulkey won three national titles at Baylor (2005, 2012, 2019) before moving to LSU, where she won a fourth in 2023.
Tara VanDerveer retired in 2024 as the winningest coach in college basketball history (men's or women's) with 1,216 victories at Stanford and Ohio State.
The first women's college basketball game was played between Stanford and Cal on April 4, 1896.
Sue Bird won two national championships at UConn (2000, 2002) and went on to become the most decorated player in WNBA history.
Candace Parker was the first woman to dunk in an NCAA Tournament game, doing so against Army in 2006.
Angel Reese's dominant rebounding at LSU helped her earn the nickname 'Bayou Barbie' and led the Tigers to the 2023 national championship.
The 2023 NCAA Women's Championship game between LSU and Iowa drew 9.9 million viewers, shattering the previous record.
Paige Bueckers was named the Naismith Player of the Year as a freshman at UConn in 2021, the first freshman to win the award since its inception in 1983.
A'ja Wilson won the 2018 national championship at South Carolina and was named the NCAA Tournament Most Outstanding Player.
Chamique Holdsclaw was a three-time national champion at Tennessee (1996, 1997, 1998) and is considered one of the greatest players in women's college basketball history.
The Naismith Women's College Player of the Year award has been given annually since 1983, with USC's Cheryl Miller winning the inaugural honor.
UConn and Tennessee played one of the greatest rivalries in sports, meeting 22 times in the regular season and 4 times in the NCAA Tournament between 1995 and 2007.
Muffet McGraw coached Notre Dame to two national championships (2001, 2018) over her 33-year tenure with the Fighting Irish.
The Wade Trophy, named after Delta State's legendary coach Lily Margaret Wade, has been awarded to the nation's best women's basketball player since 1978.
Caitlin Clark scored 3,951 career points at Iowa, finishing as the all-time leading scorer in NCAA Division I history regardless of gender.
Before Title IX, fewer than 32,000 women participated in college sports; by 2024, that number exceeded 220,000.
Tennessee's Meeks Street arena — later renamed Thompson-Boling Arena — was one of the first facilities to regularly sell out for women's basketball games in the 1990s.
The 2024 NCAA Women's Tournament generated over $388 million in ad revenue, more than triple the previous year's total.
Stanford's women's basketball program has appeared in the Final Four 14 times, one of the most by any program.
Pat Summitt earned her 1,000th career victory on February 5, 2009, with a win over Georgia.
The first women's basketball scholarship was awarded by Immaculata College in the early 1970s, helping launch competitive women's college basketball.
Immaculata College won three consecutive AIAW national championships from 1972 to 1974, becoming the first dynasty in women's college basketball.
Delta State won three consecutive AIAW titles from 1975 to 1977, led by coach Margaret Wade and star player Lusia Harris.
Lusia Harris of Delta State scored the first basket in women's Olympic basketball history and was drafted by the New Orleans Jazz in 1977.
UConn's women's team had a 90-game home winning streak from 2013 to 2019 at Gampel Pavilion and the XL Center.
The 2024 Iowa vs. LSU Elite Eight rematch drew 12.3 million viewers, making it the most-watched women's college basketball game outside of a championship.
Kelsey Plum set the single-season scoring record with 1,109 points at Washington in 2016-17.
South Carolina sold out Colonial Life Arena for every home game during the 2023-24 season, averaging over 18,000 fans per game.
The women's 3-point line was moved back from 20 feet 9 inches to 22 feet 1¾ inches for the 2021-22 season, matching the men's college distance at the time.
Baylor had a 69-game home winning streak from 2010 to 2014 under Kim Mulkey.
Caitlin Clark broke the NCAA all-time scoring record on February 15, 2024, against Michigan in front of a sold-out Carver-Hawkeye Arena.
The 2024 women's March Madness selection show was broadcast in primetime for the first time in history.
UConn has appeared in a record 23 Final Fours in women's basketball.
Tennessee was the first women's program to draw more than a million total fans in a single season, doing so in 2003-04.
Geno Auriemma was born in Italy and moved to the United States at age 7 before eventually becoming the most successful coach in women's college hoops.
Pat Summitt never had a losing season in 38 years of coaching at Tennessee.
The 2025 NCAA Women's Tournament expanded the use of team names and logos on courts and equipment, treating them more like the men's tournament.
Diana Taurasi won three consecutive national titles at UConn (2002, 2003, 2004) and was named the NCAA Tournament MOP twice.
Sabrina Ionescu of Oregon recorded 26 career triple-doubles in college, the most by any player — man or woman — in NCAA history.
The UConn-Tennessee rivalry was paused from 2007 to 2020 after a scheduling dispute between Geno Auriemma and Pat Summitt.
South Carolina's Dawn Staley is the only person to win an Olympic gold medal as a player (three times) and a national championship as a head coach.
LSU's Angel Reese had 34 consecutive double-doubles during the 2022-23 season, an NCAA record.
The 2024 Women's Final Four in Cleveland set an attendance record with 20,708 fans for the championship game.
Notre Dame's Arike Ogunbowale hit back-to-back buzzer beaters in the 2018 Final Four and championship game to lead the Irish to the title.
Skylar Diggins-Smith was a two-time All-American at Notre Dame and helped bring national attention to women's college basketball through social media.
The AP Women's Basketball Poll began in 1976, with Delta State being named the first No. 1 team.
UConn once won 111 straight games but saw the streak end on March 31, 2017, with a loss to Mississippi State in overtime at the Final Four.
Morgan William of Mississippi State hit the buzzer-beating shot that ended UConn's 111-game winning streak in the 2017 Final Four.
The women's NCAA Tournament bracket expanded from 48 to 64 teams in 1994, mirroring the men's tournament structure.
Before 1982, women's college basketball championships were run by the AIAW, not the NCAA.
Tara VanDerveer coached Stanford to two national championships (1990, 1992) and won Olympic gold as head coach of Team USA in 1996.
The 2024 season saw five women's college basketball games draw over 10 million TV viewers, unprecedented in the sport's history.
Caitlin Clark averaged 31.6 points per game during the 2023-24 season, one of the highest averages in modern NCAA history.
Rebecca Lobo led UConn to its first national championship in 1995 with a perfect 35-0 record.
The women's basketball three-point shot was introduced in the 1987-88 season, the same year the men adopted it.
Dawn Staley played point guard at Virginia from 1988 to 1992, earning three-time Naismith Player of the Year honors.
Stanford's women's basketball program produced WNBA stars Nneka Ogwumike, Chiney Ogwumike, and Candice Wiggins.
Kim Mulkey went 65-5 as a player at Louisiana Tech, winning a national championship in 1982, before returning as an assistant and then becoming a dominant head coach.
The 2023 women's NCAA Tournament saw a 63% increase in viewership compared to the previous year.
Seimone Augustus was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2006 WNBA Draft after a stellar career at LSU.
The UConn women's basketball team has had more than 40 players drafted into the WNBA.
Sheryl Swoopes of Texas Tech scored 47 points in the 1993 national championship game, still a record for a title game.
The women's shot clock was changed from 30 seconds to the current 30 seconds in 2015-16 after being increased from its original non-existence.
Title IX forced universities to provide equal athletic scholarships to men and women, directly fueling the growth of women's basketball.
Tennessee won eight national championships under Pat Summitt: 1987, 1989, 1991, 1996, 1997, 1998, 2007, and 2008.
Baylor's 40-0 perfect season in 2011-12, culminating in a national title, was one of only a handful of undefeated championship campaigns in NCAA history.
The growth of social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram has been credited with boosting interest in women's college basketball, especially among younger fans.
Caitlin Clark was named the AP National Player of the Year in both 2023 and 2024.
The 'Caitlin Clark effect' led Iowa to sell out every home game in the 2023-24 season, and road arenas often sold out when she visited.
UConn's first women's basketball game was played on January 20, 1975, a loss to Yale.
The NCAA began using the phrase 'March Madness' for the women's tournament starting in 2022, after years of separate branding.
South Carolina defeated UConn 64-49 to win the 2022 national championship, finishing with a 35-2 record.
The Nancy Lieberman Award is given annually to the nation's best point guard in women's college basketball, named after the Hall of Famer from Old Dominion.
Old Dominion won three AIAW national championships (1979, 1980) and one NCAA title (1985) behind the play of Nancy Lieberman and Anne Donovan.
Breanna Stewart was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2016 WNBA Draft after her legendary UConn career.
Maya Moore retired from the WNBA in 2019 to focus on criminal justice reform after a dominant career that began at UConn.
The first live national broadcast of a women's college basketball game was in 1978, featuring Maryland and Ohio State.
Lisa Leslie played at USC from 1990 to 1994 and later became one of the most famous women's basketball players in history.
The Wooden Award for women's basketball has been presented since 2004, with Alana Beard of Duke winning the inaugural award.
In 2021, a viral photo showed the women's NCAA Tournament weight room consisted of a single rack of dumbbells, sparking outrage and leading to immediate reforms.
Kelsey Mitchell of Ohio State scored 3,402 career points, one of the highest totals in women's Division I history.
Connecticut's Husky dog mascot, Jonathan, has been a fixture at UConn games, with the live mascot tradition dating back to 1934.
The Big 12, SEC, and Big Ten conferences have emerged as the most dominant leagues in modern women's college basketball.
The 2024 Iowa-UConn Final Four semifinal drew 14.2 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched semifinals ever.
Azzi Fudd was the No. 1 recruit in the 2021 class and chose UConn, continuing the program's tradition of landing elite talent.
The Big East Conference's women's basketball tournament has been one of the most competitive and well-attended conference tournaments.
Pat Summitt was awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2012 by President Barack Obama.
JuJu Watkins scored 42 points in her USC NCAA Tournament debut in 2024, the most by any freshman in tournament history.
The 2023 LSU national championship was the first in the program's history, led by coach Kim Mulkey and star Angel Reese.
Women's college basketball games were played in two 20-minute halves until the 2015-16 season when four 10-minute quarters were adopted.
The four-quarter format was introduced to align with international and professional play, making the game more TV-friendly.
South Carolina's Aliyah Boston won the Naismith Player of the Year in 2022 and was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2023 WNBA Draft.
The SEC has produced the most women's basketball national champions among all conferences, with Tennessee, South Carolina, LSU, and Texas A&M winning titles.
Texas A&M's stunning upset over Stanford in the 2011 national championship game was the first title for the Aggies.
Brittney Griner became the first player to dunk twice in a single NCAA game, doing so for Baylor against Florida in 2013.
The average attendance for women's college basketball games has steadily increased, with the 2023-24 season setting new records.
Caitlin Clark had 18 games of 30+ points in the 2023-24 season alone.
The ESPYS have increasingly featured women's college basketball, with Caitlin Clark winning Best College Athlete in 2024.
Notre Dame appeared in five consecutive Final Fours from 2011 to 2015 under Muffet McGraw.
Pearl Moore of Francis Marion scored 4,061 career points across all divisions, the most in women's college basketball history.
The first women's basketball game was played on March 22, 1893, at Smith College in Massachusetts.
Senda Berenson, a physical education teacher at Smith College, adapted James Naismith's rules for women, creating the first women's basketball game.
The women's game originally had nine players per side and three zones on the court; this was gradually reduced to five-on-five full court.
Full-court, five-on-five women's basketball was not universally played in college until the late 1970s.
Lynette Woodard of Kansas scored 3,649 career points from 1977 to 1981 and later became the first woman to play for the Harlem Globetrotters.
Old Dominion's Anne Donovan was a three-time All-American and was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 1995.
The women's NCAA Tournament Selection Committee was criticized in 2021 for disparities in amenities and branding between the men's and women's events.
After the 2021 controversy, the NCAA formed a Gender Equity Review that led to significant investments in women's tournament production.
The 2022 Women's Final Four in Minneapolis drew over 18,000 fans per session, a sign of the sport's growing popularity.
Stanford won the 2021 national championship in a pandemic-affected season, defeating Arizona 54-53 in the title game.
Haley Jones was named the 2021 Final Four Most Outstanding Player after leading Stanford to the championship.
Arizona's 2021 run to the championship game was a Cinderella story, as it was only the Wildcats' second Final Four appearance.
The women's basketball shot clock has changed from no clock (pre-1970), to 30 seconds (1970), and various iterations since.
South Carolina's 2024 undefeated national championship team was the first to finish 38-0, the most wins in a perfect season.
Aliyah Boston recorded a double-double in 30 straight games during the 2021-22 season, an NCAA record.
The annual women's basketball Player of the Year awards include the Naismith, Wooden, Wade, AP, and espnW honors.
UConn has had 22 players selected as All-Americans, more than any other women's basketball program.
Jennifer Rizzotti's coast-to-coast layup in the final seconds helped UConn defeat Tennessee 70-64 in the 1995 national championship.
The UConn-Tennessee series produced numerous classic games, including a 2006 overtime thriller won by UConn 74-72.
Many WNBA teams are now scouting college players earlier than ever, with freshmen like JuJu Watkins generating buzz from day one.
The 2024 WNBA Draft attracted 2.45 million viewers, driven by interest in Caitlin Clark turning pro.
Caitlin Clark was selected No. 1 overall by the Indiana Fever in the 2024 WNBA Draft.
The 2024 NCAA Women's Tournament had 48 games draw at least one million viewers, compared to just 20 in 2023.
Before the NCAA offered women's championships, women's basketball was considered a secondary sport at most universities.
The Kodak All-America team (now the WBCA All-America team) has been selecting women's basketball All-Americans since 1975.
Virginia's Dawn Staley led the Cavaliers to three Final Four appearances as a player (1990, 1991, 1992).
The Pac-12 (and its successor conferences) has produced numerous women's basketball national champions, including Stanford and USC.
Texas won an AIAW championship in 1986 under coach Jody Conradt, who is one of only five Division I coaches to reach 900 wins.
Jody Conradt coached the first undefeated championship team in NCAA women's basketball history: the 1986 Texas Longhorns at 34-0.
The 2017 Mississippi State upset of UConn in the Final Four was voted the greatest women's college basketball game ever by many media outlets.
LSU sold out the Pete Maravich Assembly Center for every home game during the 2022-23 championship season.
The women's basketball has historically been slightly smaller (28.5 inches circumference) than the men's ball (29.5 inches).
Kamilla Cardoso of South Carolina was a dominant center in the 2024 title run, recording multiple double-doubles in the tournament.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Rules Committee regularly reviews rules to improve pacing and entertainment, including proposals for a deeper three-point line.
Arike Ogunbowale scored 18 of Notre Dame's last 21 points in the 2018 championship game against Mississippi State, finishing with 35 points.
UConn played in 13 consecutive Final Fours from 2008 to 2022, a run unmatched in women's basketball.
The WNBA was founded in 1996 with eight teams, largely fueled by the talent developed in women's college basketball.
The women's game has increasingly featured dunking, though it remains relatively rare compared to men's basketball.
Tennessee's 'Lady Vols' nickname became one of the most recognizable brands in women's sports.
UConn has won six national championships at the Alamodome or in San Antonio venues.
The 2023 Final Four in Dallas set an attendance record of 19,091 for the championship game.
Tara VanDerveer's 1,216 career wins surpassed the all-time record held by Duke men's coach Mike Krzyzewski.
The 2018 Notre Dame championship was the last title for the ACC, which had been dominated by UConn in years prior.
Baylor's Lauren Cox hit a fadeaway jumper with 3.9 seconds left to defeat Oregon in the 2019 championship game.
The women's tournament bracket reveal show has evolved from a small ESPN segment to a major primetime television event.
Pat Summitt's coaching tree includes many current Division I head coaches, including Holly Warlick and Nikki Caldwell.
Geno Auriemma has coached in Connecticut since 1985 and built UConn from a little-known program into a dynasty.
The 2019 Baylor national championship team went 37-1, losing only once during the regular season.
Courtney Vandersloot holds the NCAA single-season assist record with 367 assists at Gonzaga in 2010-11.
The first nationally televised regular-season women's college basketball game was UConn vs. Tennessee on January 16, 1995.
That 1995 UConn-Tennessee game drew massive interest and helped establish women's college basketball as appointment television.
The women's basketball transfer portal has become increasingly active, with star players changing schools for NIL opportunities and better fits.
Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) deals have reshaped women's college basketball recruiting, with stars like Paige Bueckers securing major endorsements.
Before Title IX, some colleges spent as little as 1% of their athletic budgets on women's sports.
In 2024, multiple women's college basketball games outdrew men's games in TV ratings for the first time.
The Lisa Leslie Award is given annually to the best center in women's college basketball.
The Katrina McClain Award honors the best power forward in women's college basketball.
The Cheryl Miller Award goes to the best small forward in women's college basketball.
The Ann Meyers Drysdale Award is given to the best shooting guard in women's college basketball.
The Dawn Staley Award recognizes the nation's best guard, named after the legendary Virginia and South Carolina figure.
UConn's Napheesa Collier was named the AAC Player of the Year in 2019 after leading the Huskies to the Final Four.
The 2024 women's tournament saw three games with over 14 million viewers, a watershed moment for the sport.
DiJonai Carrington transferred from Stanford to Baylor and became a key player in the transfer portal era.
The women's tournament Sweet 16 has increasingly been held in NBA and WNBA arenas to accommodate larger crowds.
Louisiana Tech appeared in four of the first seven NCAA Women's Tournament championship games (1982-88).
Kim Mulkey's LSU team became the first SEC school other than Tennessee or South Carolina to win a women's national championship.
Georgia's Lady Bulldogs won the 2022 SEC Tournament and have a proud women's basketball tradition under coaches like Andy Landers.
The Ivy League has produced notable women's basketball players but has never won a national championship.
Princeton's women's basketball program has been among the most successful in the Ivy League.
Mid-major programs like Gonzaga, South Dakota State, and FGCU have made notable NCAA Tournament runs in women's basketball.
FGCU's 'Dunk City' run in the 2013 men's tournament inspired more attention for the university's women's program as well.
The Ohio State women's basketball program won the national championship in 1993 and has remained competitive under various coaches.
UConn's Paige Bueckers suffered an ACL tear in 2022, missing an entire season, and came back to lead the Huskies.
Brittney Griner's block record of 748 career blocks at Baylor may never be broken. The next closest player had around 500.
Maya Moore averaged 18.4 points per game during her UConn career and graduated with a 148-5 record.
The UConn women's basketball team's all-time record is over 1,200 wins, the best in Division I history.
Pat Summitt wrote the bestselling book 'Reach for the Summit' in 1998, inspiring athletes and coaches across all sports.
The Summitt Leadership Foundation continues Pat Summitt's legacy by funding Alzheimer's research.
The annual 'We Back Pat' week celebrates Pat Summitt's legacy and raises awareness for Alzheimer's disease.
UConn's basketball practice facility is named the Werth Family UConn Basketball Champions Center, one of the best in the sport.
The women's college basketball All-Star games, like the WBCA All-Star Game, showcase the top seniors each year.
South Carolina's women's basketball season ticket waitlist exceeded 10,000 people during the 2023-24 season.
Teresa Weatherspoon hit the iconic half-court buzzer beater for the New York Liberty in the 1999 WNBA Finals, but she started at Louisiana Tech.
Clarissa Davis of Texas was a three-time All-American (1987-89) and led the Longhorns to prominence.
Dawn Staley was an Olympic flag bearer for the USA delegation at the 2004 Athens Olympics.
The women's NCAA Tournament increased its use of LED courts and branded signage starting in 2022.
Maryland won the 2006 national championship under coach Brenda Frese, defeating Duke 78-75 in overtime.
Brenda Frese has built Maryland into a perennial contender, regularly appearing in the Sweet 16 and beyond.
Crystal Dangerfield led UConn in assists during the 2019-20 season and was drafted 16th overall by the Minnesota Lynx.
The 2002 UConn team went 39-0, one of the most dominant seasons in women's college basketball history.
The biggest blowout in NCAA Women's Tournament history was a 92-point victory by Louisiana Tech over Jackson State in 1988.
The NCAA Women's Basketball magazine has been published since the early 2000s and features previews, rankings, and analysis.
The espnW Player of the Year award has become one of the most prestigious honors in women's college basketball.
Chiney Ogwumike of Stanford was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2014 WNBA Draft and won Rookie of the Year.
Jackie Stiles of Missouri State scored 3,393 career points and led the Bears to the 2001 Final Four as an unranked team.
The 2001 NCAA Women's Tournament saw Notre Dame defeat Purdue in the championship game for the Irish's first title.
Ruth Riley's 28 points in the 2001 championship game propelled Notre Dame to its first-ever national title.
The women's college basketball landscape has shifted from a two-team (UConn-Tennessee) conversation to one with many contenders.
Texas was one of the first schools to hire a full-time women's basketball coach and invest heavily in the program.
The number of women's college basketball games broadcast on national television has more than tripled since 2015.
ESPN's 'GameDay' began regularly covering women's college basketball at marquee venues in the 2023-24 season.
The Huskies of UConn and the Gamecocks of South Carolina have emerged as the two dominant programs of the 2020s.
Connecticut won back-to-back titles in 2015 and 2016 with Breanna Stewart as the centerpiece.
The 2016 UConn team went 38-0 with an average margin of victory of 40.3 points.
Caitlin Clark led Iowa to back-to-back national championship game appearances in 2023 and 2024.
Iowa's Carver-Hawkeye Arena was expanded and renovated partly due to the surge in women's basketball attendance.
The 2024 Final Four was held at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse in Cleveland, Ohio.
The 2025 Final Four was held at Amalie Arena in Tampa, Florida.
Gabbie Marshall of Iowa was a key three-point shooter alongside Caitlin Clark during Iowa's championship runs.
Kate Martin of Iowa became a fan favorite and viral sensation during the 2024 tournament.
The growth of women's college basketball has led to increased coaching salaries, with top coaches earning over $3 million annually.
Kim Mulkey's contract at LSU was worth $32 million over 10 years, one of the richest in women's basketball history.
The SEC Network and Big Ten Network have invested heavily in women's basketball coverage, airing more games than ever before.
Baylor's Sophia Cunningham was a star at Missouri who helped bring attention to SEC women's basketball before NIL.
The women's basketball coaching carousel has become as active as the men's, with high-profile hirings making national news.
Vic Schaefer left Mississippi State for Texas in 2020, taking his defensive expertise to the Big 12.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Committee began seeding the tournament with 16 regional sites in 1994.
The women's tournament uses 'regional' names (Albany, Portland, etc.) rather than traditional compass directions for its four bracket sections.
Sylvia Hatchell coached North Carolina for 33 years and won the 1994 national championship.
Chamique Holdsclaw scored 3,025 career points at Tennessee, a program record that stood for over two decades.
The three-peat by Tennessee (1996-97-98) is one of only two in NCAA Women's Tournament history; UConn also achieved it (2002-03-04).
Tennessee's 1997 championship team is often considered one of the greatest women's basketball teams ever assembled.
The Mechi Becton-Chamique Holdsclaw-Tamika Catchings Tennessee teams of the late 1990s set the standard for program depth.
Tamika Catchings played at Tennessee from 1997 to 2001 and went on to a Hall of Fame WNBA career with the Indiana Fever.
The women's game has evolved from a half-court, slow-paced game to a fast-paced, full-court spectacle.
Most women's college basketball programs now have dedicated practice facilities, video coordinators, and nutrition staff.
USC's JuJu Watkins averaged 27.1 points per game as a freshman in 2023-24, one of the best debut seasons ever.
The 2024 championship between South Carolina and Iowa drew more viewers than many NBA playoff games.
Women's college basketball has seen a surge in merchandise sales, with team jerseys and player-branded gear selling out.
Caitlin Clark's Iowa #22 jersey was one of the best-selling college basketball jerseys — men's or women's — in 2024.
The NCAA introduced a women's basketball early signing period, allowing recruits to commit earlier in the process.
The recruiting landscape has changed dramatically, with social media and NIL making women's basketball recruits more visible.
The McDonald's All-American Game has featured a women's game since 2002, showcasing the best high school players.
The Jordan Brand Classic and Nike Hoop Summit have also added women's components.
The USA Basketball U19 team has been a proving ground for future NCAA stars.
A'ja Wilson was named SEC Player of the Year at South Carolina in 2017 and 2018.
Kelsey Plum of Washington dropped 57 points against Utah on February 25, 2017, a single-game scoring record at the time.
The women's basketball postseason also includes the WNIT and the WBI for teams that don't make the NCAA Tournament.
The Women's NIT (WNIT) has been played since 1998, giving more teams postseason opportunities.
Stanford women's basketball retired the jerseys of Jennifer Azzi, Nicole Powell, and Candice Wiggins.
Jennifer Azzi led Stanford to the 1990 national championship and was named the tournament's Most Outstanding Player.
The 1990 Stanford championship was the program's first and helped establish Tara VanDerveer as an elite coach.
The ACC has produced champions from multiple schools, including North Carolina (1994), Duke (1999 runner-up), and Notre Dame (2018).
In addition to UConn, the Big East has seen Villanova and DePaul make notable tournament runs.
DePaul's women's basketball program under Doug Bruno has been a consistent mid-major contender for decades.
The women's college basketball regular season typically runs from November to early March.
Conference tournaments are held in February and March, with champions earning automatic bids to the NCAA Tournament.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Selection Show reveals the 64-team bracket on the first Sunday after conference tournaments conclude.
The first and second rounds of the NCAA Women's Tournament are hosted on the campuses of the top 16 seeds.
The campus-site format gives higher-seeded teams a significant home-court advantage in the early rounds.
The Sweet 16 and Elite Eight rounds are held at predetermined neutral sites.
The women's Final Four has been held in some of the largest arenas in America, including the Alamodome, American Airlines Center, and Target Center.
UConn's Storrs, Connecticut campus has become a pilgrimage site for women's basketball fans.
The Gampel Pavilion in Storrs, Connecticut, has been UConn's home court since 1990 and seats over 10,000.
UConn also plays some home games at the XL Center in Hartford, which seats over 15,000 fans.
South Carolina's Colonial Life Arena in Columbia was renovated in 2016 and has become one of the toughest venues in women's basketball.
Tennessee's Thompson-Boling Arena at Food City Center seats over 21,000, one of the largest arenas used for women's basketball.
Baylor's Ferrell Center was replaced by the Foster Pavilion in 2023, giving the Bears a state-of-the-art home.
The women's game adopted a wider lane (12 feet) matching the men's court dimensions.
The Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame in Springfield, Massachusetts, has inducted many women's college basketball legends.
Pat Summitt was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 2000.
Geno Auriemma was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 2006.
The 1996 Olympic gold-medal U.S. women's team featured college legends like Sheryl Swoopes, Lisa Leslie, and Rebecca Lobo.
Rebecca Lobo's 35-0 UConn season in 1995 and her role on the 1996 Olympic team helped popularize women's basketball in America.
The first women's basketball game at Madison Square Garden was played in 1975 between Immaculata and Queens College.
Rutgers' C. Vivian Stringer coached for 50 years across Cheyney State, Iowa, and Rutgers, compiling over 1,000 victories.
C. Vivian Stringer led three different schools to the Final Four — Cheyney State (1982), Iowa (1993), and Rutgers (2007).
The 1982 championship game, Louisiana Tech vs. Cheyney State, was the first official NCAA Women's Tournament final.
Connecticut's Kara Wolters was the 1997 Naismith Player of the Year and a key player on the Huskies' 1995 title team.
The women's basketball assistant coaching market has become highly competitive, with salaries exceeding $250,000 at top programs.
Title IX compliance requires schools to demonstrate equitable participation opportunities, financial assistance, and treatment for women's sports.
The NCAA gender equity report released in 2021 found a $13.5 million spending gap between the men's and women's basketball tournaments.
Following the 2021 report, the NCAA committed to closing the gap and making the women's tournament experience equitable.
The 2023 NCAA contract with ESPN valued women's basketball at over $100 million annually for the first time.
Women's college basketball analytics have grown significantly, with programs using advanced stats like offensive and defensive efficiency.
Her Hoop Stats is a website dedicated to advanced analytics in women's basketball, covering college and professional levels.
The AP Top 25 women's basketball poll is voted on by sportswriters and broadcasters and released weekly during the season.
UConn held the No. 1 ranking in the AP Poll for a record 252 consecutive weeks from 2008 to 2017.
Dawn Staley was named the Naismith Coach of the Year in 2020 and 2022.
The Women's Basketball Coaches Association (WBCA) has over 4,500 members and advocates for the growth of the sport.
The Kay Yow Cancer Fund, named after the legendary NC State coach who died of breast cancer in 2009, raises awareness during the 'Pink Zone' games.
Kay Yow coached NC State for 34 years and guided the Wolfpack to the 1998 Final Four.
The 'Pink Zone' initiative has raised millions of dollars for breast cancer research through women's college basketball.
Iowa set a women's college basketball attendance record when 55,646 fans watched the Hawkeyes play at Kinnick Stadium in an exhibition.
The outdoor basketball game at Kinnick Stadium on October 15, 2023, was called 'Crossover at Kinnick.'
The record attendance demonstrated the enormous demand for women's college basketball experiences.
LSU's Hailey Van Lith transferred from Louisville and became a key player for the Tigers in the 2023-24 season.
The transfer portal has allowed players to find better fits and has increased parity in women's college basketball.
Multiple women's basketball games during the 2024 tournament were played in NBA arenas that seated over 18,000 fans.
The growth of women's college basketball has created more professional opportunities, with leagues in Europe, Australia, and Asia signing college grads.
The Big Ten Conference has historically featured powerhouses like Ohio State, Iowa, Maryland, and Indiana in women's basketball.
Indiana's women's basketball program reached the Final Four in 2022 for the first time in program history under coach Teri Moren.
Virginia Tech made its first Final Four in 2023 under coach Kenny Brooks, a sign of growing parity in the sport.
The Patriot League, America East, and other smaller conferences have produced occasional NCAA Tournament upsets.
Harvard's women's basketball team won the 2024 Ivy League title and earned an NCAA Tournament bid.
The 2025 NCAA Women's Tournament is expected to continue the viewership growth trajectory set by the 2024 edition.
Caitlin Clark's half-court and deep three-point shots became social media sensations, going viral across platforms.
NBC began airing select women's college basketball games in 2024, joining ESPN and CBS in broadcasting the sport.
The 2024-25 season saw average viewership of women's college basketball games increase by over 100% compared to two years earlier.
Flau'jae Johnson of LSU combined her basketball career with a music career, becoming one of the most marketable players in NIL.
The women's college basketball coaching profession has become one of the highest-paid in women's sports.
The SEC led all conferences in women's basketball attendance in the 2023-24 season.
South Carolina's Dawn Staley has been praised for building a team-first culture that emphasizes defense and depth.
The 2023 South Carolina team held opponents to 55.4 points per game, one of the best defensive marks in modern history.
The Big 12 Conference expanded in 2024, adding schools like BYU, Houston, and Cincinnati, broadening its women's basketball reach.
Conference realignment has reshaped women's college basketball, with traditional powers now competing in new leagues.
The 2024 Pac-12 dissolution sent Stanford, USC, and UCLA to the ACC and Big Ten, changing the competitive landscape.
Oregon's women's basketball program rose to prominence under Kelly Graves, reaching the 2019 Final Four.
Sabrina Ionescu's triple-double against Stanford in the 2019 Pac-12 Tournament is one of the most iconic performances in conference tournament history.
The women's college basketball referee pool has expanded and professionalized, with officials earning higher fees.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Officiating Improvement Program was created to enhance the quality and consistency of officiating.
The 'Gamecocks' of South Carolina draw their nickname from the state's Revolutionary War history, not from roosters.
The women's college basketball schedule now includes non-conference tournaments in the Bahamas, Hawaii, and other destination locations.
The Women's Basketball Invitational (WBI) has provided additional postseason exposure for teams since 2009.
South Carolina's Zia Cooke was a key guard on the 2022 championship team and later joined the WNBA.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament has used the 'First Four' play-in format since 2022 to expand to 68 teams.
California's women's basketball program, coached by Charmin Smith, has been rebuilding and reaching for NCAA Tournament appearances.
The growth of women's basketball podcasts and digital media has created a large and engaged online community.
The Full Court Press podcast and 'Around the Rim' are popular sources for women's college basketball coverage.
Ticket prices for women's tournament games have surged, with Final Four tickets commanding over $200 on the secondary market.
The average margin of victory in UConn's 111-game winning streak was 39.7 points.
During UConn's 111-game streak, the team was pushed to overtime just once.
The women's college basketball recruiting class rankings are published by ESPN, Prospects Nation, and other outlets.
UConn has signed the No. 1 recruiting class multiple times, including years that brought in Paige Bueckers and Azzi Fudd.
The five-star recruit system in women's basketball mirrors the men's system and is increasingly scrutinized by fans and media.
The WBCA Coaches' Poll is another major ranking system alongside the AP Poll in women's college basketball.
Baylor's NaLyssa Smith was the 2022 Big 12 Player of the Year and was drafted second overall in the WNBA Draft.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament Most Outstanding Player (MOP) award has been given since 1982, with Louisiana Tech's Janice Lawrence winning the first.
Breanna Stewart is the only player to win four consecutive Final Four MOP awards (2013-2016).
Stanford's Ogwumike sisters (Nneka and Chiney) both went No. 1 overall in their respective WNBA Drafts (2012 and 2014).
The 2022 national championship was the first for South Carolina's Gamecocks under Dawn Staley, capping a dominant 35-2 season.
Women's college basketball attendance topped 11 million total fans for the first time during the 2023-24 season.
The women's basketball 'Rivalry Week' on ESPN features marquee matchups and has become a staple of the midseason schedule.
The SEC-Big Ten Challenge in women's basketball has showcased top conference matchups since its inception.
The Phil Knight Legacy and Phil Knight Invitational tournaments have increasingly featured women's basketball brackets.
The Battle 4 Atlantis women's tournament in the Bahamas has become a premier early-season event.
Many women's college basketball programs now have NIL collectives that help players secure endorsement deals.
Paige Bueckers signed a reported seven-figure NIL deal with Gatorade while still at UConn.
The NCAA has introduced mental health initiatives specifically for women's college athletes, including basketball players.
Women's basketball student-athletes graduate at higher rates than men's basketball players at most Division I schools.
The graduation success rate for women's basketball players in Division I was 92% in the 2023 report.
Dawn Staley was the first Black coach to win multiple women's basketball national championships.
The 1998 Tennessee championship team, featuring Chamique Holdsclaw, Tamika Catchings, and Semeka Randall, is often called the greatest women's college team ever.
Tamika Catchings was born with a hearing impairment but overcame it to become one of the greatest basketball players in history.
The women's college basketball media days have grown from small affairs to events covered by national outlets.
ESPN's Holly Rowe, Andraya Carter, and Rebecca Lobo are among the most prominent women's college basketball commentators.
The 2007 Tennessee national championship was Pat Summitt's seventh and the program's first in nine years.
Candace Parker scored 17 points and grabbed 9 rebounds in Tennessee's 2007 championship game victory over Rutgers.
The women's basketball coaching carousel often sees coaches leave mid-major programs for Power Five opportunities.
The espnW Impact 25 list annually recognizes the most influential women in sports, frequently featuring college basketball figures.
Pat Summitt's 'Definite Dozen' rules for success have been adopted by coaches and leaders in many fields beyond basketball.
UConn's 2010 team went 39-0, winning by an average of 35.4 points per game, one of the most dominant seasons ever.
The women's basketball Hall of Fame in Knoxville, Tennessee, was founded in 1999 and inducts players, coaches, and contributors.
Elena Delle Donne played at Delaware and was the No. 2 overall pick in the 2013 WNBA Draft, becoming a two-time league MVP.
The 2008 Tennessee championship team featured Candace Parker and defeated Stanford in the title game.
Pat Summitt published her memoir 'Sum It Up' in 2013, detailing her battle with Alzheimer's disease.
The women's basketball game ball is 28.5 inches in circumference, one inch smaller than the men's ball, to accommodate hand size differences.
In 2024, the NCAA committed to providing the women's tournament with the same level of branding, including the 'March Madness' trademark on all promotional materials.
Oregon State reached the 2016 Final Four under coach Scott Rueck, a major achievement for the Beavers program.
Mississippi State reached back-to-back championship games in 2017 and 2018, losing both to UConn and Notre Dame respectively.
Tina Charles won the 2010 Naismith Player of the Year at UConn and was the No. 1 overall WNBA Draft pick.
The women's three-point shooting percentage has averaged around 33-35% in recent NCAA seasons.
Free throw shooting in women's college basketball historically averages about 70-72%, slightly higher than the men's game.
The longest game in NCAA Women's Tournament history went to triple overtime, with Connecticut defeating Ohio in 2009.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Committee uses metrics like the NET rankings, strength of schedule, and quad records to select and seed tournament teams.
The NET (NCAA Evaluation Tool) replaced the RPI as the primary ranking tool for tournament selection in 2018-19.
UConn and South Carolina have combined to win 6 of the last 10 women's national championships.
The evolution of the women's game has made it faster, more athletic, and more entertaining, driving unprecedented fan interest.
Title IX celebrated its 50th anniversary on June 23, 2022, with women's basketball as one of its most visible success stories.
Before Title IX, many universities did not offer women's basketball as a varsity sport.
Geno Auriemma's coaching record at UConn is over 1,200 wins with a winning percentage above .880.
The 2019 Baylor championship featured a dominant defensive team that held opponents to under 56 points per game.
Kalani Brown of Baylor was a dominant center whose interior presence anchored the 2019 championship team.
The Super 16 reveal for the women's tournament now gets significant TV coverage and social media buzz.
The women's basketball exhibition season allows teams to play two exhibition games before the regular season begins.
Many coaches credit the 2024 viewership boom as a tipping point that will permanently elevate women's college basketball.
The NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament trophy is the same design as the men's trophy, symbolizing parity in the sport.
Caitlin Clark's partnership with Nike included a signature shoe deal, a rarity for college basketball players.
Iowa's Caitlin Clark had 41 points and 12 assists in a single NCAA Tournament game against LSU in 2024, one of the greatest individual performances in tournament history.
The SEC dominated the 2024 NCAA Tournament with four teams in the Elite Eight.
Angel Reese's 'you can't see me' gesture toward Caitlin Clark in the 2023 championship game became one of the most iconic moments in tournament history.
Women's college basketball has produced Olympic gold medalists in every Summer Games since 1976.
The U.S. women's Olympic basketball team has won gold in every Olympics since 1996, largely stocked with former college stars.
The 2004 Olympic team featured Diana Taurasi, Sue Bird, and Tamika Catchings — all products of elite college programs.
Women's basketball was first included as an Olympic sport at the 1976 Montreal Games.
Becky Hammon, who starred at Colorado State, became the first woman to serve as an acting NBA head coach in 2021.
